seoTitle: Create and Print Checks in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Draft, queue, align, and batch-print vendor checks in QuickBooks Online without wasting pre-printed check stock on misaligned runs.
intro: Printing physical checks for vendor payments in QuickBooks Online involves more than just writing the amount — getting your printer alignment right the first time saves you from ruining check stock. This guide walks through creating a check, queuing it for printing, and using the visual alignment wizard.

steps.heading: Create the check entry
steps.body: Click the + New button in the upper-left corner, then choose Check under the Vendors column.

steps.heading: Fill in payee and expense details
steps.body: Select your payee, pick the checking account, and map the payment to the correct expense category.

steps.heading: Queue it for printing
steps.body: Check the Print later box in the upper-right of the form instead of assigning a check number manually, then save and close.

steps.heading: Open the print queue
steps.body: Return to the + New menu and select Print checks under the Vendors column to see your pending batch.

steps.heading: Align your printer and print
steps.body: Use Print setup to run a test page on plain paper, adjust alignment against your check stock, then select your pending rows, enter the starting check number, and print.

faq.question: Why did I need to check the Print later box?
faq.answer: Checking Print later funnels the check into the master batch tray instead of assigning it a check number immediately, which is required for batch printing.

faq.question: What if my printed checks don't line up with the pre-printed stock?
faq.answer: Run a test page on plain paper, lay it over your check stock, and use the alignment wizard to adjust the grid coordinates before printing for real.

faq.question: Can I print multiple checks in one batch?
faq.answer: Yes, the print queue lets you select multiple pending rows and print them together in a single run.

faq.question: Where do I set the starting check number?
faq.answer: The starting check number field appears in the print queue right before you click Preview and print.
